2604.12477.title: Mining Large Language Models for Low-Resource Language Data: Comparing Elicitation Strategies for Hausa and Fongbe
2604.12477.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are trained on data contributed by low-resource language communities, yet the linguistic knowledge encoded in these models remains accessible only through commercial APIs. This paper investigates whether strategic prompting can extract usable text data from LLMs for two West African languages: Hausa (Afroasiatic, approximately 80 million speakers) and Fongbe (Niger-Congo, approximately 2 million speakers). We systematically compare six elicitation task types across two commercial LLMs (GPT-4o Mini and Gemini 2.5 Flash). GPT-4o Mini extracts 6-41 times more usable target-language words per API call than Gemini. Optimal strategies differ by language: Hausa benefits from functional text and dialogue, while Fongbe requires constrained generation prompts. We release all generated corpora and code.
2604.12477.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）是基於低資源語言社群貢獻的數據進行訓練的，但這些模型中編碼的語言知識仍然只能通過商業API訪問。本文探討了策略性提示是否能從LLMs中提取可用的文本數據，針對兩種西非語言：豪薩語（Afroasiatic，約8000萬說話者）和豐貝語（Niger-Congo，約200萬說話者）。我們系統地比較了兩個商業LLM（GPT-4o Mini和Gemini 2.5 Flash）中的六種引導任務類型。GPT-4o Mini每次API調用提取的可用目標語言單詞數是Gemini的6-41倍。最佳策略因語言而異：豪薩語受益於功能性文本和對話，而豐貝語則需要受限生成提示。我們將釋放所有生成的語料庫和代碼。
2604.12477.author: Mahounan Pericles Adjovi et.al.
2604.12477.authors: Mahounan Pericles Adjovi, Roald Eiselen, Prasenjit Mitra
